steps.title: Find the shared input $c$
steps.content: Since $p$ is linear with slope $8$, write $p(x)=8x+b$. Use $p(5)=42$: $$8(5)+b=42,$$ so $b=2$. Then use $p(c)=-6$: $$8c+2=-6,$$ so $c=-1$.

steps.title: Fit the line for $t$
steps.content: Now $t(c)=-7$ means $t(-1)=-7$, and the other point is $t(6)=56$. The slope of $t$ is $$\frac{56-(-7)}{6-(-1)}=\frac{63}{7}=9.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The slope of $y=t(x)$ is $9$, which matches D. By hand, the same slope is $\dfrac{56-(-7)}{6-(-1)}=\dfrac{63}{7}=9$.
